meta.note: Official manuals are summarized with a pointer to the full PDF in Box. APEC field notes carry the full content (spelling normalized). Never guess part numbers — items marked [?] must be verified before ordering.

entries.title: BP pinpad IP change after trigger pull (READ FIRST)
entries.summary: Upgrading BP to latest version changes the pinpad IP scheme after the Commander trigger pull — you must re-IP the pinpads. Includes BP EMV config IPs and Commander EF access notes.
entries.keywords: bp buypass trigger pull pinpad ip change subnet commander ef passport 911tech table load emv ca public key download pop
facts: Verify in Commander first: Payment Controller > EPS > EPS Global > POP
EMV config: Pumps 192.168.8.x offset 101 · Media 192.168.6.2 with Mako (not .51/.52 — verify others) · Server 192.168.9.30 (NIC A IP used because of default route, instead of 192.168.31.11)
Commander EF: Commander connects on port 2 of the 24-port switch; only 1 Verifone-zone port — may need a temporary switch
Commander EF: create secure user 'Passport' (password 911Tech) with all roles; run all pulls from Passport
Register path: POS > More > More > Tools > Sign on > 91 > Setup > Network > BP > EPS Function > Initiate Trigger Pull / Perform Table Load / Perform EMV CA Public Key Download
entries.body:
Configure trigger pull parameters, then call BP and initiate the trigger pull. It loads all the Buypass configuration — you may still have to change some settings (codec, terminal ID and brand). Reboot the Commander, then call Buypass and perform the table load, then download the EMV CA file.

After any upgrade to the latest version, BP changes the pinpad IPs after the trigger pull (old 192.168.9.31+ /255.255.255.192 to new 192.168.9.8+ /255.255.255.224) and you have to change them in the pinpads. Always check the Commander configuration before changing an IP: Payment Controller > EPS > EPS Global Configuration > POP.

entries.title: Citgo site setup — trigger pull, LAN configs (ControlScan vs non-ControlScan), mobile pay
entries.summary: Citgo configuration differs by MNSP: ControlScan (Paysafe) vs anything else (Cybera/Mako/Acumera). Network will NOT be online before the trigger pull (table download). Includes LAN/route tables from the embedded screenshots, trigger-pull settings, Citgo Mobile Pay config and FIS back-office info.
entries.keywords: citgo trigger pull table download emv pdl controlscan paysafe cybera mako acumera lan route citgodns kickback loyalty cgo.cards fis club citgo mobile pay echosat petroparms heartland
facts: Table download: CSR Function > Network Menu > EPS Network Functions > Trigger Pull Function > Initiate Trigger Pull; then Download EMV PDL (apply table takes ~30 sec)
LAN (both types): NIC A (isolated payment) 192.168.40.250, GW 192.168.40.1, mask 255.255.255.0
Routes (NON-ControlScan — Acumera/Cybera/Mako): host 192.168.40.250; network 8.8.0.0; network 63.241.252.0/255.255.255.0; network 65.118.49.0/255.255.255.0 — all via 192.168.40.1
Loyalty (Kickback Rewards): host 192.168.41.2 via 192.168.40.1

Trigger Pull (Payment Controller > EPS Global > Trigger Pull Configuration): Merchant ID = 000 + site's 8-digit CITGO ID (11 digits) · Company ID 009 · Terminal ID 08 non-ControlScan / 10 ControlScan · timeouts 2/5 min · Remove Pending + Display Alarm checked

Service End Point: non-ControlScan https://petroparms.prod.e-hps.com/getUpdateAvailable.svc · ControlScan https://HPS-Trigger.echosat.net · passcode from Heartland Helpdesk (leave default, auto-updates)
Mobile pay route: host 34.192.165.152 via 192.168.40.1 (service 'Mobile Pay')

CITGO Mobile Pay (VFI Mobile V2): program 'CITGO Mobile Pay', merchant ID format 0311-2036, auth 'Scan Token', host cgo.cards port 7261 SSL, heartbeat 45 sec, Send Loyalty Details checked, outdoor pre-auth timeout 30 sec
Club Citgo configuration: check the manual
entries.body:
Two main differences to take into account during Citgo configuration: it depends on whether the MNSP is ControlScan or not. ControlScan has its own configuration; anything else uses the other configuration. The network will not be online before the trigger pull (table download).

Table download steps: 1) CRS Function > Network Menu > EPS Network Functions > Trigger Pull Function > Initiate Trigger Pull (table validates successfully). 2) CRS Function > Network Menu > EPS Network Functions > Download EMV PDL. Sequence on screen: DOWNLOADING TRIGGER PULL > SUCCESS > APPLY TABLE DOWNLOAD STARTED (wait ~30 sec) > APPLY TABLE SUCCESS > then run EMV PDL.

entries.title: Buypass generic (unbranded) with Cybera — startup & FEP config
path: 2_Input_data/Loose Batch 2/POS/buypass generic unbranded with cybera/bypass generec with CYBERA START UP.docx
entries.summary: Full startup for an unbranded Buypass site behind Cybera: site must be ONLINE before table download; Cybera must configure the 325 switch for Verifone POS with URL not IPs (enable Datawire). Includes port map, LAN config, Buypass FEP settings from the annotated screenshots, and how to read a Buypass ID.
entries.keywords: buypass generic unbranded cybera datawire fdcnet srsxml umf codec dealer id var sheet terminal tpp rve044 7735 table load emv ca 325 switch allow registration cashback
facts: Ports: WAN1 internet · 10 payment NIC A · 9 Commander · 11 reg101 · 12 pinpad 126 · 13 reg102 · 14 pinpad 127 · 7 EMV pump DCR · 8 media · 1234 back office
LAN NIC A: 192.168.40.250, GW 192.168.40.1, mask 255.255.255.0, DNS 8.8.8.8/8.8.4.4 — Default Route MUST be checked for Cybera payment hosts (NIC-A)

Buypass FEP (Payment Controller > EPS > Buypass Configuration): Codec ALWAYS UMF · Dealer ID from VAR sheet (5 or 6 digits, no leading zeros) · Terminal ID 001/002/003 from VAR sheet · TPP ID RVE044 (grayed) · EPS IP 192.168.31.31 port 7735 · heartbeat 120
FEP parameters: Call for Authorization + Visa/MC/Amex/Discover small credit enabled · Brand = BUYPASS · cashback prompts 5/10/20/40
Security: Datawire Secure Channel CHECKED · web address https://prod.dw.us.fdcnet.biz/sd/srsxml.rc · NOTE: if 'Allow Registration' keeps unchecking itself, that is expected after registration
Go-live: 1) confirm Cybera configured switch with URL 2) ask Buypass to reset the Datawire connection 3) reboot sometimes required; check Diagnostics > Host Status online
Once online: CRS Function > Network Menu > EPS Network Functions > PERFORM TABLE LOAD, then > PERFORM EMV CA PUBLIC KEY FILE DOWNLOAD
Reading a Buypass ID 'RB13 078909 001': RB = Datawire RA IPs · 078909 = Dealer ID · 001 = Terminal ID. Cybera does Datawire; Paysafe does IPs
Datawire URL: https://prod.dw.us.fdcnet.biz/sd/srsxml.rc
entries.body:
Important: site has to be online before table download. Ask Cybera to configure the 325 switch with Verifone POS and URL, not IPs (enable Datawire).

Once the network is online (check Diagnostics > Host Status), do the table download: 1) CRS Function > Network Menu > EPS Network Functions > Perform Table Load. 2) CRS Function > Network Menu > EPS Network Functions > Perform EMV CA Public Key File Download.

entries.title: Installing / replacing a pinpad — APEC procedure
entries.summary: APEC's 12-step pinpad install/replacement sequence, then the ConfigClient EPS steps.
entries.keywords: pinpad install replace viperpay os push dept key contactless 192.168.31.126 port 5000 pop emv initialize
facts: 1. Connect pinpad to power + USB only — NOT to the Verifone zone yet
2. Push latest OS (e.g. 30251300-A100, comes with hotfix A100) · 3. reboot
4. Push ViperPay compatible with the Commander software
5. Check IP 192.168.31.126, change to .127/.128... as needed
6. Time reset + reboot time · 7. push contactless file · 8. reboot
9. Push dept key · 10. reboot · 11. 'right-left-right-left' menu · 12. re-check IP and port 5000
ConfigClient: Payment Controller > EPS Configuration > EPS Global Configuration > POP tab — set pinpad model, IP, port 5000
Initialize EMV if the site runs EMV inside

entries.title: Pinpad master note — MX915 & M400 full installation
entries.summary: The full pinpad software stacks. MX915: OS, vpay, CL file, debit key, image file (BP), VHQ file (BP + ExxonMobil), R-L-R-L 69900 menu, IP + port 5000. M400: complete kernel 7.0.3 install sequence from the Verifone Premier package, plus IP scheme, hidden menu, reboot times.
entries.keywords: mx915 m400 p400 viperpay evpay vipa kernel 703 vos2 adkcrt vhq petrohome heartbeat shell bp exxon hidden menu #6#6#6 rlrl 69900 reboot 3am ssl rfid brick 30620500
facts: MX915 stack: OS · vpay · CL file · debit key · image file for BP · VHQ file for BP and ExxonMobil · access menu R L R L 69900 · IPs + port 5000

M400 kernel 7.0.3 (only on M400/P400 running EVPAY 2.03.03 R04): 1) EVPAY-2.06.00-r04-Kernel-703-Engage-FullInstall-Petrohome-Prod.zip 2) OS dl.vos2-prod-Engage-release-31612700.tgz 3) dl.adkcrt-201527.tgz 4) PETRO_VIPA_6.9.0.8_engage-ProdSigned.tgz 5) Engage_Evpay-2.06.00-r04-Kernel 703--prodsigned.tgz 6) accept SAM agreement for VHQ 7) Vhqconfig_3.2.7_Engage_petrohome-12561.tgz (or customer-specific)
WARNING: installing current OS over OS 30620500 or lower without the step-up version can brick the device (VASC Field Service Bulletin 0322_002)
EPS: POP IP must equal M400 IP — ConfigClient > Payment Controller > EPS Config > EPS Global > POP tab; then EMV Initialization
All brands same steps EXCEPT Shell (own package). BP + ExxonMobil use their own VHQ files — do NOT load petrohome; must show successful heartbeat
Static IP example: 192.168.031.126 / mask 255.255.255.000 / GW 192.168.031.031 / DNS 8.8.8.8, 8.8.4.4 · BP and Shell have different IP schemes
IP tricks if it will not take: ensure ethernet plugged · set pinpad-1 IP first then change to pinpad-2 · or skip and configure later from hidden menu
Set 24-hour reboot: 3:00 AM for .126, 3:30 for .127, etc.
Hidden menu: #6#6#6 · verify SSL enabled (NOT for Shell) · verify RFID enabled · reboot

entries.title: ExxonMobil register errors — reports & mailbox clears
entries.summary: How to clear the recurring ExxonMobil register messages: 'Report Available', 'Mobile OOB Report Available', and 'Unsecured Mailbox is Full / Unsecured Mail Available'.
entries.keywords: exxon exxonmobil register error report available mobile oob unsecured mailbox mail print total loyalty discount
facts: 'Report Available': CSR Function > Network Menu > EPS Network Report > List Available Totals Report (print), then > Print Total Report (enter the report ID)

'Mobile OOB Report Available': CSR Function > Network Menu > Mobile Reports > Report by Host (print all previous reports before current), and > Above Site Loyalty Report > Loyalty Discount By Type Report (print all previous)
'Unsecured Mailbox is Full' / 'Unsecured Mail Available': CSR Function > Network Menu > EPS Network Function > Print Mail Messages

entries.title: Configure Client — Commander backup, screenshots & post-upgrade checklist
entries.summary: APEC's ConfigClient workflow: what to verify before an SMSimportexport backup, which screens can NOT be captured in the backup (take screenshots), forecourt/DCR setup, initialization order, and post-upgrade checks.
entries.keywords: configclient config client smsimportexport backup screenshots lan eps trigger pull managed modules mobile pos dcr dukpt fuel blend variable 120 electrical 2 initialization order emv table download mako 6600 version about
facts: Before SMSimportexport backup: verify the APEC account has full access in ConfigClient
Screenshot (not in backup) — LAN: Initial Setup > LAN Config: 1) isolated payment NIC (NIC A) 2) device-specific routes (may be multiple pages) 3) DNS

Screenshot — EPS: Chevron/Buypass/Sunoco = Payment Controller > EPS Config > (brand) Configuration + EPS Global > EPS · Citgo/Marathon = EPS Global > Trigger Pull · BP = BOTH trigger pull and BP Configuration
Screenshot — Managed modules (Tools > Managed Modules > system resource) · Mobile payment (Payment Controller > Mobile Payment Config: site + host) · POS (Payment Controller > POS Config > POS)
After Commander software update, check: site landline phone number · site ZIP code · site service ID
DCR drivers: Tools > Managed Module > Current Config > DCR drivers > Advanced: encryption type DUKPT (+ other options) · also Fuel drivers
Forecourt: DCR Config > DCR positions · DCR Keys (4 rows x 5 columns, assign positions to pumps) · DCR idle screen
Fuel config: check 'allow access' per fueling position · Wayne dispensers: blend is VARIABLE 120 · Gilbarco: blend is ELECTRICAL 2
Initialization ORDER: Forecourt > Initialization: 1-Fuel · 2-Fuel driver · 3-DCR driver · 4-DCR (in order)
Network online check: CSR Function > Network > Diagnostic Check Host Status; if online do table download with the brand, reboot after
Before table download initialize EMV: Payment Controller > EPS Config > EPS Global > EMV Initialization
Commander software version: register CSR Function > Help > About, or ConfigClient Help > About
Isolated payment NIC for Mako 6600 + 24-port switch (Chevron): IP 192.168.32.11 · GW 192.168.32.31 · mask 255.255.255.0

entries.title: Media box (DFS DX) startup & test sequence
entries.summary: APEC's quick sequence to stand up a DFS media box (Edge) and verify it is talking to the dispensers.
entries.keywords: media box dfs dx edge dxmonitor dxpromo set gold sitecode device agent rdm 2111 netstat dispenser communicating
facts: Power the media box, connect monitor, fix the IPs, connect to the switch
Run Edge network tool utility to test the ports
Run DXMonitor as admin · DXPromo as admin · prompt: run 'set gold' to check DxM/DxPromo versions
TeamViewer for remote · fix date and time on the Edge
Windows env vars: Edit system environment > Environment Variables > New: SITECODE + site number
Services: 'Device Agent' service running · RDM services running
netstat -a: port 2111 lines show which dispensers are communicating

entries.title: VAR sheet — what it is
entries.summary: A VAR (Value-Added Reseller) sheet is the file with the key merchant information used for payment processing — it supports communication between the site's payment gateway and the merchant account. Dealer IDs and Terminal IDs for Buypass FEP config come off the VAR sheet. PCS = Petroleum Card Services.
entries.keywords: var sheet value added reseller merchant dealer id terminal id pcs petroleum card services

entries.title: Chevron unconfirmed transaction log — check BEFORE any software change / Commander reload
entries.summary: For ALL POS systems: check the Unconfirmed Transaction Log (secure) before changing any software, including upgrades. If a Chevron Commander is reloaded/replaced without submitting the unconfirmed log, the site loses that money. Full RMC submission procedure (revised by Shiraz Tharani).
entries.keywords: chevron unconfirmed log ucl rmc secure reports terminal batch detail pending ems purge offline data day close reload replace commander get paid 877-243-8457 shiraz
facts: Chevron RMC (to get paid for unconfirmed charges): 1-877-243-8457 · RMC fax 866-743-1160 · Verifone POS help desk 1-888-777-1891

Daily: after Close Day print Terminal Batch Detail Report — CSR > 2. Network Menu > 4. EPS Network Reports > 1. Terminal Batch Detail Report; unconfirmed transactions must be re-printed from EPS SECURE Reports with full card number and sent to Chevron
EPS Secure Reports login: ID = 1 · password = the manager's ConfigClient password

Before reload/replacement (in order): 1) Day close (note the day number) 2) Pending EMS Detail Report (Network Menu > EPS Network Reports > 4) — print 2 copies, one for customer one for the APEC office (Peggy) 3) Purge Offline Data (Network Menu > EPS Network Functions > 9) — moves pending EMS to unconfirmed log 4) EPS Secure Reports > Terminal Batch Detail Report (use day number; 2 copies; note ALL batch numbers credit + debit) 5) EPS Secure Reports > Network Batch Detail Report for every batch (2 copies)
Submit ALL printed reports to Chevron and call RMC to validate before reloading the site controller
Reference: VASC bulletin 0923_001 'Chevron Requirements for Verifone Site Controller Reloads or Replacements' (Premier > Manage > Petro Downloads > General VASC Info > VASC Bulletins)

entries.title: Murphy USA Pop & Drop — per-site VLAN/IP list (118 sites)
entries.summary: Per-site network numbers for the Murphy USA dispenser-replacement (Pop & Drop) project: VLAN 112 payment subnet (/26 — first usable = start IP, last usable = gateway = DNS), VLAN 111 store subnet (/24), and fuel server IP. Full table in the body — search the Murphy site number.
entries.keywords: murphy usa pop drop pop&drop vlan 112 111 fuel server ip subnet site number dispenser replacement musa
facts: Pattern: VLAN 112 = 10.20x.x.x/26, mask 255.255.255.192, start = network+1, GW/DNS = last usable
Fuel server = 10.225.x.x per site
Source: 'IP Address for Pop & Drop 12.18.25' — open the xlsx in Box for formulas

entries.title: 2nd MPPA setup — technician instructions (Verifone rollout, Nov 2022)
entries.summary: Add the 2nd MPPA (mobile payment processing application) network: Cybera sites add route host 34.200.98.89 via Cybera GW 192.168.32.14 (service 'siteconnector'); all sites add a 2nd MPPA host in Mobile Payment Config > Host Configuration; validate online after reboot. Longer instruction doc for sites already on EMR+ (<15 min).
entries.keywords: mppa second host 34.200.98.89 siteconnector cybera exxonmobil rollout mobile
facts: Cybera-only route: host 34.200.98.89 GW 192.168.32.14 mask /32 service siteconnector
Then Payment Controller > Mobile Payment Config > Host Configuration > ADD 2nd MPPA
Validate online: Tools > reboot Commander first if needed
